subject: Changes to EventManager
commit_message:
Changes to EventManager

Added event manager and operation manager. Deleted New files

Issue-ID: POLICY-1266
Change-Id: I5d32ef6a8a7aa1dda86c3f21fa1650f4954c3abc
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>

Patch Set 3:

This build is failing because of duplicate aai code in both /policy/drools-applications and /policy/models. Once this issue gets resolved, the build should work fine, given that it build successfully on my local machine.

Patch Set 3:

I don't think is duplicate code Pramod.  I see the errors below that are from a review that has not been merged, so they are yet not seen by this review.  

One question though, then do we want two copies for example a ControlLoopOperationManagerNew.java and a ControlLoopOperationManager.java (with and without the "New" suffix).

I have created these "New" classes, because they significantly differ from the old classes which support A&AI named query. I kept it that way so that we have a back up plan if we decided to revert back to named query (there was some discussion regarding this in one of the SO ONAP calls). Replacing the old files with the "New" files is extremely trivial and probably will take no more than 15 mins on my side. But if I do that, reverting back to named query can be difficult.

Patch Set 3:

> This is the review I was referring to ..
 > 
 > https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/84469/

You are correct, that review is not merged that's why I am getting these errors.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

I don't believe we want 2 versions of a class. We already have a big enough mess on our hands with this code base. Use one class that can support either query. Use a boolean flag.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

> I don't believe we want 2 versions of a class. We already have a
 > big enough mess on our hands with this code base. Use one class
 > that can support either query. Use a boolean flag.

I have finished this task. For all my changes in at both /policy/models and /policy/drools-applications, I have removed the "New" files and moved their methods to the original files. I have also added a Boolean flag, which can decide whether to use the old named queries or the new custom queries.
